--- Thread (50 messages, 2018-01-24) ---
[2018-01-24 14:55] mtanzer: playing real players is probably the fastest way to learn, although it will also involve losing a lot of games
[2018-01-24 14:55] steel0229e (Steel): I think it is a great tool to teach you to think fast in 45s games
[2018-01-24 14:55] redrame: Yeah but if you only want to win games then play anything else
[2018-01-24 14:55] steel0229e (Steel): I agree it reinforces really bad habits so it is not a way to improve
[2018-01-24 14:56] steel0229e (Steel): but if you play a lot, you get used to all the units
[2018-01-24 14:57] redrame: The way you learn the game is by mimicking top players until you understand their thought process and then improving on it or finding holes
[2018-01-24 14:57] steel0229e (Steel): and you get smashes less than by playing ladder where you get crushed until you ragequit
[2018-01-24 14:57] redrame: That involves a lot of losing but you get it out of the way upfront
[2018-01-24 14:58] redrame: Master bot just delays all the losing and gives you false confidence
[2018-01-24 14:58] steel0229e (Steel): but beating it is already a high accomplishment
[2018-01-24 14:58] redrame: Dunning Kruger is not something you wanna deal with in this game
[2018-01-24 14:59] steel0229e (Steel): and I think the majority of new palyers are not used to the difficulty of this game so I'd not recommend ladder to anyone
[2018-01-24 14:59] steel0229e (Steel): not in the current circumstances
[2018-01-24 14:59] redrame: You want to get constantly reminded that you’re bad until you’re good
[2018-01-24 14:59] steel0229e (Steel): we need a lot of new palyers for ladder to be an option to learn
[2018-01-24 14:59] steel0229e (Steel): rigtht now you get crushed and you dont even know what you did wrong
[2018-01-24 15:00] redrame: Yeah so master bot is there to get a concept of the game
[2018-01-24 15:00] redrame: And then you play other players to see why they’re better at it than you
[2018-01-24 15:00] mtanzer: I played mbot a lot more and ladder a lot less when I was newer.  I think it just depends what the player prefers.  if the goal is to get good asap then real players will be better, but that may be a less fun experience for many.  also I agree with steel that mbot is good for when you're still getting used to what all the units do / how they work
[2018-01-24 15:00] redrame: Yeah most people probably need a compromise
[2018-01-24 15:01] redrame: I didn’t even beat master bot once before playing and got my ass kicked
[2018-01-24 15:01] redrame: But I also rose up way more quickly than anyone else I know
[2018-01-24 15:02] crash_overlord: I beat Elyot the first day I played prismata
[2018-01-24 15:03] crash_overlord: Then again, it's elyot so not like it means anything :Swade:
[2018-01-24 15:03] steel0229e (Steel): I beat Lightning the first day I played prismata... because he disconnected Kappa

messages.content: and two drones -> two animi -> three tarsiers -> amporilla

messages.author: awaclus
messages.content: not sure if I'm already making the mistake there or if it's something later on

messages.author: mtanzer
messages.content: hmm so a 3 tarsier amporilla is ok, but if you could get another tarsier or two first that'd be really good.  Like in actual pris games you can sometimes just barely pull off a 4 tarsier ampo and that just overpowers your opponent, I usually aim for at least 4 when I go for it

messages.author: awaclus
messages.content: so that means I shouldn't DD/AA/TTT right?

messages.author: mtanzer
messages.content: the line I just won with is a little different from the two you said if that helps

messages.author: awaclus
messages.content: because amporilla is just better than getting another three tarsiers there

messages.author: mtanzer
messages.content: not sure I follow your last statement.  I think the problem with DD/AA/TTT is getting the second animus that early just to buy one tarsier the next turn isn't great

messages.author: p0lari
messages.content: the rules for normal games don't really apply in the challenge

messages.author: p0lari
messages.content: I don't remember the exact unit composition I used but I felt the key thing was managing your supplies and what you block with and when

messages.author: mtanzer
messages.content: true.  I guess the point I wanted to make is that a 3 tarsier ampo is hardly better than just buying more tarsiers both ingame and in the challenge

messages.author: mtanzer
messages.content: and the fact that it requires another animus usually makes it worse

messages.author: p0lari
messages.content: since you're facing a lot of damage with bad defenders and limited rhino supply once grid drops, you need to get a little more non-prompt defense than your intuition would suggest

messages.author: awaclus
messages.content: I've never run out of rhino supply

messages.author: awaclus
messages.content: I never have enough gold to buy so many rhinos

messages.author: mtanzer
messages.content: I guess one tip for this challenge, which also helps with a lot of the challenges, is try to make sure your first 5 turns or so are all very efficient.  for example the two lines you gave...DD/AA/TTT and A/TTD/TA/ampo both waste a red t3
